conflict: Nick must convert vague sabotage intention into a specific executable method, which requires understanding a system well enough to break it
consequence: Vague sabotage intention becomes specific testable plan — the method requires Nick to pursue promotion even harder, deepening the contradiction
must_land_beats: Discovery of the calibration-anchor system — how senior evaluators' ratings weight the entire pipeline's learning
Nick's test corruptions: small, deliberate misratings that the system absorbs without flagging
The painting metaphor — bad pigment mixed into primer, invisible until the wall blooms
The realization that guardrails catch random noise and laziness, not systematic expert-level sabotage
pressure_source: Nick must convert vague sabotage intention into a specific executable method, which requires understanding a system well enough to break it

setups_to_plant.description: The specific mechanism of Nick's sabotage — calibration anchors, compounding errors, the system's blind spot for expert-level systematic corruption. Must be concrete enough that the reader understands the method but told through Nick's painter vocabulary, not CS jargon

state_shift: Vague sabotage intention becomes specific testable plan — the method requires Nick to pursue promotion even harder, deepening the contradiction
